title: Ethics & Professional Responsibility
summary: How PMI expects a project manager to act with responsibility, respect, fairness, and honesty. In PMBOK 8 this maps to the Be an Accountable Leader principle (which merges stewardship and leadership): be a diligent, trustworthy, and caring custodian of people, resources, and decisions. Built into scan-friendly blocks: the four Code values, hard boundaries, scenario -> right action, and a decision path.

sections.heading: Ethics is the Be an Accountable Leader principle
sections.body: Ethics is not a side topic. In PMBOK 8 it maps directly to Be an Accountable Leader, the principle that merges stewardship and leadership: be a diligent, respectful, and caring steward acting with integrity toward people, resources, the organization, and the environment. The PMI Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct gives the baseline, and its four values - Responsibility, Respect, Fairness, Honesty - are what the exam tests.

blocks.title: How to read an ethics question
lines: Ethical action = tell the truth early, protect confidential information, avoid self-interest, follow lawful and organizational process, escalate serious concerns through the right channel.
The best answer is transparent, lawful, respectful, and focused on long-term trust over short-term comfort.
If one option hides information to keep someone happy and another communicates facts responsibly, choose the transparent one.
Accountable leadership = act with integrity even when it is uncomfortable.

sections.heading: PMI Code of Ethics values
sections.body: The Code of Ethics rests on four values. Each supports the Be an Accountable Leader principle. Read the value, then the behavior the exam rewards.

groups.label: Means
lines: Take ownership for decisions and actions.
Accept the consequences.
Honor commitments.
Comply with laws, regulations, policies, and professional standards.

groups.label: On projects
lines: Accurate reporting and proper escalation.
Careful use of authority.
Address problems instead of ignoring them.
Report unethical or illegal conduct through the proper channel.

groups.label: Right answer
lines: PM owns the issue, uses the approved process, and addresses the risk directly.
Document facts, assess impact, follow process, escalate - do not cover up, blame, or bypass governance.

groups.label: Means
lines: Treat others with dignity.
Listen to different viewpoints.
Engage professionally even during conflict.
Cultural awareness and confidentiality - not passive politeness.

groups.label: On projects
lines: Adapt communication across cultures without stereotyping.
Seek understanding before judging behavior.
Protect sensitive business, personal, and procurement information.
Disclose only to those authorized with a legitimate need to know.

groups.label: Right answer
lines: Responds to a cultural misunderstanding with curiosity, not blame.
Unethical if it embarrasses someone publicly, ignores cultural context, or discloses confidential information without authority.

groups.label: Means
lines: Decide impartially and objectively.
Avoid favoritism, discrimination, and self-serving behavior.
Apply consistent standards based on facts.

groups.label: On projects
lines: Disclose and manage conflicts of interest.
Use approved criteria and governance under pressure.
Applies to procurement, staffing, feedback, and issue resolution.

groups.label: Right answer
lines: Discloses the conflict and returns to objective process.
Not the option that protects a sponsor, friend, or vendor by bypassing process or granting unfair advantage.

groups.label: Means
lines: Understand the truth and act truthfully.
Provide accurate information in a timely way.
No deception, half-truths, or misleading omissions.

groups.label: On projects
lines: Report status honestly even when the news is unwelcome.
Honest about estimates, expertise, defects, and causes of variance.
Say what is known, what is unknown, and what will be done next.

groups.label: Right answer
lines: Combines truth with professionalism: communicate, document, plan the response.
Eliminate any option that improves the appearance of the project without improving the facts.

sections.heading: Hard boundaries you never cross
sections.body: These lines are absolute regardless of pressure, schedule, or who is asking. On the exam, any option that violates one of these is wrong.

blocks.title: Never falsify or manipulate
lines: No sugar-coating, no hiding risks, no manipulating metrics, no delaying bad news to protect reputation.
Adjust tone for professionalism, never substance for appearance.
Revising wording to make performance look better than it is = deception. Eliminate it.

blocks.title: Always disclose conflicts of interest
lines: If your judgment could reasonably be questioned, disclosure comes before action.
Declare real or potential conflicts through the proper organizational mechanism; remove yourself from the decision if appropriate.
Silence is not neutrality. Even if you believe you can stay objective, an undisclosed conflict erodes trust.

blocks.title: No bribes, no improper gifts
lines: Do not accept gifts that may affect judgment or grant preferential treatment.
Do not steer work to a favored supplier outside a fair process.
Do not offer or take a bribe or kickback under any circumstance.

blocks.title: Protect confidentiality and IP
lines: Never distribute restricted, private, or regulated information to unauthorized parties - even when a powerful stakeholder asks.
Respect copyrights, patents, licenses, trade secrets, and usage restrictions; do not misrepresent ownership.
Transparency does not mean sharing everything with everyone.

blocks.title: Obey law, regulation, and mandatory policy
lines: Do not approve or join actions that break legal, safety, procurement, privacy, or regulatory requirements.
Legal and regulatory compliance outranks schedule pressure, budget pressure, and stakeholder convenience.
When one option is faster but noncompliant and another is slower but lawful, the lawful option is the professional choice.

sections.heading: Scenario -> right action
sections.body: Map the exam trigger to the ethical response.

rows.key: Stakeholder asks you to soften a bad status report
rows.value: Keep the facts accurate. Adjust tone, not substance. Report status, impact, and next steps.

rows.key: You have a financial, personal, or relational interest in a decision
rows.value: Disclose the conflict through the proper channel; step out of the decision if judgment may be compromised.

rows.key: A relative's or friend's company could win the contract or job
rows.value: Declare the relationship; use fair, objective criteria; do not proceed informally.

rows.key: Offered a gift that could sway your judgment
rows.value: Decline; do not let it influence the decision; follow policy on disclosure.

rows.key: Powerful stakeholder demands confidential or restricted data
rows.value: Protect it. Share only with those authorized and with a legitimate need to know.
Respond with curiosity and professionalism; seek understanding, do not assign blame.

rows.key: A faster option requires breaking a law, rule, or safety standard
rows.value: Stop the improper action, document facts, consult the proper authority, follow compliance channels.

rows.key: Credible evidence of fraud, harassment, bribery, or falsified reporting
rows.value: Document objective facts, preserve confidentiality, escalate through the designated channel.

rows.key: Team does not yet know the answer
rows.value: State what is known, what is unknown, and the plan to resolve it. Do not fabricate certainty.

sections.heading: When facing an ethical dilemma
sections.body: Work the steps in order. This keeps you inside Responsibility and Honesty at the same time: act on credible concerns, but through the right structure.

items.title: Gather the facts
lines: Document objective facts and the impact on scope, schedule, cost, quality, safety, compliance, or trust.
Do not investigate beyond your authority.

items.title: Check law and policy first
lines: Is anything illegal or against mandatory policy?
Compliance and safety outrank convenience and pressure.

items.title: Disclose any conflict
lines: If your objectivity could be questioned, declare it before acting.
Step out of the decision if appropriate.

items.title: Protect confidentiality
lines: Share information only with authorized parties who have a legitimate need to know.

items.title: Communicate honestly
lines: State facts, impacts, and next steps.
Professional tone, unaltered substance.

items.title: Escalate through the proper channel
lines: Sponsor, PMO, compliance, HR, legal, ethics hotline, or internal audit as policy directs.
Not gossip, retaliation, or private side deals.

items.title: Act now only for genuine emergencies
lines: Immediate safety or legal emergencies justify urgent intervention ahead of the normal channel.

blocks.title: Reporting reminder
lines: Do not conceal the issue, and do not jump straight to a public accusation.
Preserve confidentiality while you escalate.
Responsibility and honesty work together: act on credible concerns, through the right structure.

items.text: PMBOK 8 principle (merges stewardship and leadership): diligent, respectful, caring custodian who acts with integrity. Ethics lives here.
Personal interest could interfere, or appear to interfere, with professional judgment. Disclose and manage it.
Disclose sensitive information only to authorized parties with a legitimate need to know.
Respect copyrights, patents, licenses, trade secrets, and usage restrictions; do not misrepresent ownership.
Sponsor, PMO, compliance, HR, legal, ethics hotline, or internal audit - the defined route for reporting concerns.
Truthful, timely communication of facts and impacts. Not the same as sharing everything with everyone.

blocks.title: One-line summary
blocks.text: Be the honest, fair, respectful, and responsible steward: tell the truth early, disclose conflicts, protect confidences, obey the law, and escalate through the right channel.

source: Drawn from the PMP Examination Content Outline, the PMI Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct, and the PMBOK Guide (Be an Accountable Leader principle), aligned to PMBOK 8.
